title: Habenaria graminea
intro: Habenaria graminea, also known as Indian snake root or grassy orchid, is a species of orchid found in tropical and subtropical regions. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which can help to treat conditions such as fever, cold, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Habenaria graminea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Habenaria graminea include decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are often used to treat respiratory and skin disorders.
taxonomy: Habenaria graminea is classified as a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The genus Habenaria is a group of terrestrial orchids, which are commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. Habenaria graminea is further classified as a terrestrial orchid, meaning it grows in the ground rather than in trees or epiphytically. It is a member of the subfamily Orchidoideae, which includes many species of terrestrial orchids.
